gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The **budget ledger** — one enforcement mechanism over the separate named budgets (RFC-0014 §8"] # [doc = " resolved). An effect with **no** budget set cannot consume anything (default tightly scoped, I5):"] # [doc = " you opt into a broader effect by explicitly [`set`](Budgets::set)ting its budget."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The env-machine threads a `&mut Budgets` (alongside its fuel/depth clocks) and the recovery driver"] # [doc = " consumes the *same* type, so an overrun surfaces as [`EvalError::EffectBudget`] on the one runtime"] # [doc = " refusal path (RFC-0014 §4.8). The concurrency wave (RFC-0008) layers *per-task* ledgers on this seam."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " [`EvalError::EffectBudget`]: crate::EvalError::EffectBudget"] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Default , PartialEq , Eq)] pub struct Budgets { remaining : BTreeMap < EffectKind , u64 > , }

gaps.snippet: impl Budgets { # [doc = " An empty ledger — no effect may run until a budget is declared (I5)."] # [must_use] pub fn new () -> Self { Budgets { remaining : BTreeMap :: new () , } } # [doc = " Builder: declare a budget."] # [must_use] pub fn with (mut self , budget : EffectBudget) -> Self { self . set (budget) ; self } # [doc = " Declare (or reset) a budget for its effect kind."] pub fn set (& mut self , budget : EffectBudget) { self . remaining . insert (budget . kind () , budget . amount ()) ; } # [doc = " The remaining budget for `kind` (`None` if none was declared)."] # [must_use] pub fn remaining (& self , kind : & EffectKind) -> Option < u64 > { self . remaining . get (kind) . copied () } # [doc = " Consume `amount` of `kind`'s budget. An overrun — including consuming an effect with **no**"] # [doc = " declared budget — is an explicit, graceful [`EffectBudgetExhausted`] (I4). On success the"] # [doc = " remaining budget is decremented."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " Returns [`EffectBudgetExhausted`] when `amount` exceeds the remaining budget (or none is set)."] pub fn consume (& mut self , kind : EffectKind , amount : u64) -> Result < () , EffectBudgetExhausted > { let remaining = self . remaining . get (& kind) . copied () . unwrap_or (0) ; if amount > remaining { return Err (EffectBudgetExhausted { kind , requested : amount , remaining , }) ; } self . remaining . insert (kind , remaining - amount) ; Ok (()) } }

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; use crate :: EvalError ; # [test] fn an_undeclared_budget_refuses_immediately () { let mut b = Budgets :: new () ; let err = b . consume (EffectKind :: Retry , 1) . unwrap_err () ; assert_eq ! (err . kind , EffectKind :: Retry) ; assert_eq ! (err . remaining , 0) ; } # [test] fn a_declared_budget_drains_then_overruns_explicitly () { let mut b = Budgets :: new () . with (EffectBudget :: Attempts (2)) ; assert ! (b . consume (EffectKind :: Retry , 1) . is_ok ()) ; assert_eq ! (b . remaining (& EffectKind :: Retry) , Some (1)) ; assert ! (b . consume (EffectKind :: Retry , 1) . is_ok ()) ; let err = b . consume (EffectKind :: Retry , 1) . unwrap_err () ; assert_eq ! (err . requested , 1) ; assert_eq ! (err . remaining , 0) ; } # [test] fn an_io_budget_can_be_primed_drained_and_overruns_explicitly () { let mut b = Budgets :: new () . with (EffectBudget :: Ops (1)) ; assert_eq ! (b . remaining (& EffectKind :: Io) , Some (1)) ; assert ! (b . consume (EffectKind :: Io , 1) . is_ok ()) ; let err = b . consume (EffectKind :: Io , 1) . unwrap_err () ; assert_eq ! (err . kind , EffectKind :: Io) ; assert_eq ! (err . remaining , 0) ; } # [test] fn named_budgets_are_keyed_by_name_and_independent () { let net = EffectKind :: Named ("net" . to_owned ()) ; let disk = EffectKind :: Named ("disk" . to_owned ()) ; let mut b = Budgets :: new () . with (EffectBudget :: Named ("net" . to_owned () , 2)) . with (EffectBudget :: Named ("disk" . to_owned () , 0)) ; assert_eq ! (b . remaining (& net) , Some (2)) ; assert ! (b . consume (net . clone () , 2) . is_ok ()) ; assert ! (b . consume (net , 1) . is_err ()) ; assert ! (b . consume (disk , 1) . is_err ()) ; } # [test] fn an_overrun_routes_through_the_runtime_eval_error_channel () { let mut b = Budgets :: new () . with (EffectBudget :: Depth (0)) ; let exhausted = b . consume (EffectKind :: Cascade , 1) . unwrap_err () ; let as_eval : EvalError = exhausted . clone () . into () ; assert_eq ! (as_eval , EvalError :: EffectBudget (exhausted)) ; } # [test] fn effect_kind_display_is_non_empty_and_variant_specific () { assert_eq ! (EffectKind :: Retry . to_string () , "retry") ; assert_eq ! (EffectKind :: Alloc . to_string () , "alloc") ; assert_eq ! (EffectKind :: Io . to_string () , "io") ; assert_eq ! (EffectKind :: Cascade . to_string () , "cascade") ; assert_eq ! (EffectKind :: Time . to_string () , "time") ; let named = EffectKind :: Named ("myeffect" . to_owned ()) . to_string () ; assert ! (named . contains ("myeffect") , "Named variant must include the effect name; got: {named:?}") ; } # [test] fn effect_budget_exhausted_display_contains_kind_and_amounts () { let e = EffectBudgetExhausted { kind : EffectKind :: Retry , requested : 3 , remaining : 1 , } ; let msg = e . to_string () ; assert ! (msg . contains ("retry") , "Display must include the effect kind 'retry'; got: {msg:?}") ; assert ! (msg . contains ('3') , "Display must include the requested amount 3; got: {msg:?}") ; assert ! (msg . contains ('1') , "Display must include the remaining amount 1; got: {msg:?}") ; } }
